name: Murui Huitoto

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Murui Huitoto (muru1274).

notes: Huitotoan language (Murui/Bue) of Colombia and Peru, with an orthography and phonology drawn from Wojtylak's descriptive grammar. Six vowel qualities each contrast for length (written doubled); ɨ is a high back unrounded vowel. The letter j is /h/, z is a dental fricative, y is a voiced affricate and ñ a palatal nasal; /s/ occurs mainly in Spanish loans. The high vowels also surface as glides word-initially. A pitch/tone contrast that the practical spelling does not mark, together with stress, is left unencoded, setting the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Murui Huitoto (muru1274)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
